name: Grace
role: Small business owner running her own e-commerce site and office IT
backstory: Grace runs a small online retail business with 5 employees. She set up an Ubuntu server in the office closet to run Nextcloud for file sharing and a Gitea instance for her developer contractor. She followed a blog post to set it up 6 months ago but now Nextcloud is throwing 502 errors after an unattended apt upgrade ran overnight. She has no IT staff and limited Linux experience beyond the initial setup.
emotional_triggers: Employees unable to access shared files during business hours
Fear of losing data from a bad fix attempt
Being told to 'just reinstall' when data is at stake
existing_strengths: Has SSH access and remembers basic navigation commands
Keeps regular backups on an external drive (rsync cron job from the blog post)
Methodical — will follow steps exactly as given
Knows her Nextcloud admin password and server IP
success_looks_like: Nextcloud is back online for her team, she understands what went wrong, and she has a plan to prevent surprise breakage from unattended upgrades.
